segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: The last full day, day five, is for some people the highlight of the holiday, with a ride on the steam railway, from Douglas to Port Erin.
segments.zh: 最后一天，也就是第五天，对有些人来说是这次旅行的亮点，乘坐蒸汽火车从道格拉斯到伊林港。探索一番后，大巴会带你去俯瞰马恩岛小岛的海岬，那是离海岸不远的一个小岛。从那里继续前往卡斯尔敦，它曾是马恩岛的首府，还有它的中世纪城堡。

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: After some time to explore, a coach will take you to the headland that overlooks the Calf of Man, a small island just off the coast.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: From there you continue to Castletown, which used to be the capital of the Isle of Man, and its mediaeval castle.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: And on day six it's back to the ferry – or the airport, if you flew to the island – and time to go home.
segments.zh: 第六天，返回渡轮——或者如果你飞过来的话就去机场——然后该回家了。

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Now I'd like to tell you ...
segments.zh: 现在我想告诉你们……

segments.speaker: RUTH
segments.en: Ed, how are you getting on with the reading for our presentation next week?
segments.zh: 埃德，我们下周演示要用的阅读材料你读得怎么样了？

segments.speaker: ED
segments.en: Well, OK, Ruth – but there's so much of it.
segments.zh: 嗯，还行，露丝——但材料太多了。

segments.speaker: RUTH
segments.en: I know, I hadn't realised birth order was such a popular area of research.
segments.zh: 我知道，我没想到出生顺序是个这么热门的研究领域。

segments.speaker: ED
segments.en: But the stuff on birth order and personality is mostly unreliable.
segments.zh: 但关于出生顺序和性格的那些东西大多不可靠。从我读到的来看，很多关于你在家庭中的位置决定某些性格特征的说法只是刻板印象，没有可靠证据支持。

segments.speaker: ED
segments.en: From what I've been reading a lot of the claims about how your position in the family determines certain personality traits are just stereotypes, with no robust evidence to support them.

segments.speaker: RUTH
segments.en: OK, but that's an interesting point – we could start by outlining what previous research has shown.
segments.zh: 好吧，但这是个有趣的观点——我们可以先概述一下之前的研究成果。有些研究可以追溯到一百多年前。

segments.speaker: RUTH
segments.en: There are studies going back over a hundred years.

segments.speaker: ED
segments.en: Yeah – so we could just run through some of the typical traits.
segments.zh: 是啊，我们可以快速过一遍一些典型特征。比如，大家普遍认为老大通常适应能力较差，因为他们永远无法接受弟弟妹妹的到来。

segments.speaker: ED
segments.en: Like the consensus seems to be that oldest children are generally less well-adjusted because they never get over the arrival of a younger sibling.

segments.speaker: RUTH
segments.en: Right, but on a positive note, some studies claimed that they were thought to be good at nurturing – certainly in the past when people had large families they would have been expected to look after the younger ones.
segments.zh: 对，但积极的一面是，有些研究表明他们很擅长照顾人——当然，在过去人们孩子多的时候，老大通常被期望照顾弟弟妹妹。

segments.speaker: ED
segments.en: There isn't such a clear picture for middle children – but one trait that a lot of the studies mention is that they are easier to get on with than older or younger siblings.
segments.zh: 对于中间的孩子，情况就没那么清晰了——但很多研究提到的一个特点是，他们比哥哥姐姐或弟弟妹妹更容易相处。

segments.speaker: RUTH
segments.en: Generally eager to please and helpful – although that's certainly not accurate as far as my family goes – my middle brother was a nightmare – always causing fights and envious of whatever I had.
segments.zh: 通常他们乐于取悦他人、乐于助人——但就我家的情况来说，这绝对不准确。我中间的弟弟简直是个噩梦，总是挑起争端，还嫉妒我拥有的一切。

segments.speaker: ED
segments.en: As I said – none of this seems to relate to my own experience.
segments.zh: 就像我说的，这些似乎都跟我的经历不符。我是家里最小的，但我在读过的任何研究里都看不到自己的影子。按理说我应该是个善于社交、自信、容易交朋友的孩子——但实际上我特别害羞。

segments.speaker: ED
segments.en: I'm the youngest in my family and I don't recognise myself in any of the studies I've read about.

segments.speaker: ED
segments.en: I'm supposed to have been a sociable and confident child who made friends easily – but I was actually terribly shy.

segments.speaker: RUTH
segments.en: Really?
segments.zh: 真的吗？真有意思。关于双胞胎的研究有数百项，但大多是关于后天培养与先天遗传的……

segments.speaker: RUTH
segments.en: That's funny.

segments.speaker: RUTH
segments.en: There have been hundreds of studies on twins but mostly about nurture versus nature ...

segments.speaker: ED
segments.en: There was one on personality, which said that a twin is likely to be quite shy in social situations because they always have their twin around to depend on for support.
segments.zh: 有一项关于性格的研究说，双胞胎在社交场合可能非常害羞，因为他们总是有双胞胎兄弟姐妹在身边可以依靠。

segments.speaker: RUTH
segments.en: My cousins were like that when they were small – they were only interested in each other and found it hard to engage with other kids.
segments.zh: 我的表兄弟姐妹小时候就是这样——他们只对彼此感兴趣，很难和其他孩子交往。不过现在他们挺好的。

segments.speaker: RUTH
segments.en: They're fine now though.

segments.speaker: ED
segments.en: Only children have had a really bad press – a lot of studies have branded them as loners who think the world revolves around them because they've never had to fight for their parents' attention.
segments.zh: 独生子女的名声一直不太好——很多研究给他们贴上了孤僻的标签，认为他们觉得世界围着自己转，因为他们从来不需要争夺父母的关注。

segments.speaker: RUTH
segments.en: That does seem a bit harsh.
segments.zh: 这听起来有点苛刻。我之前没考虑过的一类是那些有年长很多的兄弟姐妹的孩子——有几项研究提到，这些孩子成长得更快，并且被期望自己完成基本的事情，比如穿衣服。

segments.speaker: RUTH
segments.en: One category I hadn't considered before was children with much older siblings – a couple of studies mentioned that these children grow up more quickly and are expected to do basic things for themselves – like getting dressed.

segments.speaker: ED
segments.en: I can see how that might be true – although I expect they're sometimes the exact opposite – playing the baby role and clamouring for special treatment.
segments.zh: 我能理解这可能是真的——虽然我猜他们有时恰恰相反，扮演小宝宝的角色，吵着要特殊待遇。

segments.speaker: RUTH
segments.en: What was the problem with most of these studies, do you think?
segments.zh: 你觉得这些研究大多有什么问题？

segments.speaker: ED
segments.en: I think it was because in a lot of cases data was collected from only one sibling per family, who rated him or herself and his or her siblings at the same time.
segments.zh: 我认为是因为在很多情况下，数据只来自每个家庭中的一个兄弟姐妹，他们同时给自己和兄弟姐妹打分。

segments.speaker: RUTH
segments.en: Mmm.
segments.zh: 嗯。不过，一些关于出生顺序和学业成绩之间关系的旧研究已被证明是准确的。智力测试的成绩从长子/女到其弟弟妹妹会略有下降。这已在许多近期研究中得到证实。

segments.speaker: RUTH
segments.en: Some of the old research into the relationship between birth order and academic achievement has been proved to be accurate though.

segments.speaker: RUTH
segments.en: Performances in intelligence tests decline slightly from the eldest child to his or her younger siblings.

segments.speaker: RUTH
segments.en: This has been proved in lots of recent studies.

segments.speaker: ED
segments.en: Yes.
segments.zh: 是的。不过很多研究没有考虑到家庭规模。兄弟姐妹越多，家庭社会经济地位越低的可能性就越大——这也可以解释兄弟姐妹在学业表现上的差异。

segments.speaker: ED
segments.en: Although what many of them didn't take into consideration was family size.

segments.speaker: ED
segments.en: The more siblings there are, the likelier the family is to have a low socio-economic status – which can also account for differences between siblings in academic performance.

segments.speaker: RUTH
segments.en: The oldest boy might be given more opportunities than his younger sisters, for example.
segments.zh: 例如，最大的男孩可能比他的妹妹们得到更多机会。

segments.speaker: ED
segments.en: Exactly.
segments.zh: 没错。

segments.speaker: RUTH
segments.en: But the main reason for the marginally higher academic performance of oldest children is quite surprising, I think.
segments.zh: 但我觉得，老大学习成绩略好一点的主要原因其实挺让人意外的。这不仅仅是因为他们小时候多受了些关注，智力上更有优势——我原本也这么想。而是因为他们通过把过程说出来，给弟弟妹妹当老师，自己也从中受益。

words.def: 用言语表达（verbalise的现在分词）

segments.speaker: RUTH
segments.en: It's not only that they benefit intellectually from extra attention at a young age – which is what I would have expected.

segments.speaker: RUTH
segments.en: It's that they benefit from being teachers for their younger siblings, by verbalising processes.

segments.speaker: ED
segments.en: Right, and this gives them status and confidence, which again contribute, in a small way, to better performance.
segments.zh: 对，这给了他们地位和自信，这些又反过来对成绩有一点小小的促进作用。

segments.speaker: RUTH
segments.en: So would you say sibling rivalry has been a useful thing for you?
segments.zh:
那你觉得兄弟姐妹间的竞争对你有好处吗？
我觉得有——我弟弟特别烦人，我们老打架，但这让我变得更坚强了。我知道怎么保护自己。我们吵过很凶的架，我宁愿死也不肯跟他道歉——但我们得忍受彼此，大多数时候还是能和睦相处的。

segments.speaker: RUTH
segments.en: I think so – my younger brother was incredibly annoying and we fought a lot but I think this has made me a stronger person.

segments.speaker: RUTH
segments.en: I know how to defend myself.

segments.speaker: RUTH
segments.en: We had some terrible arguments and I would have died rather than apologise to him – but we had to put up with each other and most of the time we co-existed amicably enough.

segments.speaker: ED
segments.en: Yes, my situation was pretty similar.
segments.zh: 对，我的情况也差不多。但我觉得有两个哥哥并没有让我变得不那么自私——我从来不愿意让他们用我的东西……

segments.speaker: ED
segments.en: But I don't think having two older brothers made me any less selfish – I was never prepared to let my brothers use any of my stuff ...

segments.speaker: RUTH
segments.en: That's perfectly normal, whereas ...
segments.zh: 这完全正常，而……

pairs.note: 题干说'最大的孩子'，原文说'照顾更小的孩子'，通过照顾对象暗示说话者就是老大。

paraphrase.explain: 题干问'最大的孩子'，原文提到'照顾更小的孩子'，暗示说话者就是老大，从而定位到G选项。

pairs.note: 题干说middle child，原文说middle brother，都是指中间的孩子。

paraphrase.explain: 题干问中间孩子，原文提到middle brother，直接对应定位。

pairs.note: 题干说'最小的孩子'，原文说'家里最小的'，意思相同，但表达不同。

paraphrase.explain: 题干问最小的孩子，原文定位句说'我是家里最小的'，直接对应。

pairs.note: 题干和原文完全一致，直接定位。

paraphrase.explain: 题干关键词 'a twin' 在原文中直接出现，可快速定位到该句。

pairs.note: 题干中的'an only child'对应原文开头的'Only children'，是原词重现。

paraphrase.explain: 题干'an only child'在原文第一句以'Only children'出现，直接定位到答案所在句。

items.prompt: a child with much older siblings

pairs.q: a child with much older siblings
pairs.note: 题干和原文几乎完全一致，只是单复数变化，直接定位。

paraphrase.explain: 题干中的 'a child with much older siblings' 在原文中几乎原样出现为 'children with much older siblings'，因此可以直接定位到该句。

items.prompt: What do the speakers say about the evidence relating to birth order and academic success?

pairs.q: account for differences between siblings in academic performance
pairs.p: account for differences between siblings in academic performance
pairs.note: 原文直接说明家庭规模等因素可以解释学业差异，对应题干中的'evidence'。

paraphrase.explain: 题干问关于出生顺序与学业成功的证据，原文直接提到'evidence relating to birth order and academic success'，并说明家庭规模等因素可以解释差异，因此定位到该句。

items.prompt: What does Ruth think is surprising about the difference in oldest children's academic performance?

pairs.note: 原文和题干中都是'surprising'，直接对应。

pairs.q: difference in oldest children's academic performance
pairs.p: marginally higher academic performance of oldest children

paraphrase.explain: 定位句开头提到'main reason... is quite surprising'，与题干问'surprising about the difference'对应，因此答案在定位句后文。

items.prompt: Which TWO experiences of sibling rivalry do the speakers agree has been valuable for them?

pairs.note: valuable 和 useful 是同义替换，都表示‘有益的’

pairs.p: sibling rivalry has been a useful thing
pairs.note: 题干中的 experiences 被替换为 thing，但核心是 sibling rivalry 和 useful

paraphrase.explain: 通过定位句中的 'sibling rivalry has been a useful thing' 与题干 'sibling rivalry has been valuable' 对应，找到讨论价值的部分。

items.prompt: Which TWO experiences of sibling rivalry do the speakers agree has been valuable for them?

pairs.note: valuable 与 useful thing 同义替换，表示‘有益的’
原文直接重复题干关键词，帮助定位

paraphrase.explain: 通过题干中的‘sibling rivalry’在原文中直接出现，以及‘valuable’替换为‘useful thing’，可以快速定位到对话开头部分。
